reviews.review_title: Awesome but worth it for the price?
reviews.review_description:
✨ l-i-n-k-t-r*e-e/k-e-l-l-0-1-5 ✨

(Remove - and add a point instead of a * to get a surprise! Got a bunch of cozy/sim/adventure/casual games, go take a look if you're interested!)

I love this game, the graphics are amazing, the colors of the landscapes depending on the time of the day, this is a very chill experience in Japanese, right in front of Mount Fuji. Is it worth it though? Because this game is incredibly short, and the price is quite high for the content offered.
For me, it was worth it, because I love this anime, I love very chill games and I know I will go back to it from time to time, but I would not suggest this game for everyone. If you are just looking for a chill experience with a good amount of content, I would advise you to get another game.

This felt like an episode from the anime from everything like the real voice actors to the narrator describing how to cook food. And it includes little details like the pine cone that makes a noise. 
I really hope they add in more content. As of the time of me writing this (April 4, 2021), the game $21, but feels like a polished demo. I could return the game, but I won’t as I really hope they add in more content, since as of today, this game isn’t on the official store yet.

I can appreciate the effort the devs put into this experience, that with all the cast from the anime series, this feels like a canon episode. The graphics were also made to make it close to the anime. There is not a lot you can do in this title, though, mostly just point and click with your line of sight, and the exploration around the lake lacking. A lot of the actions are just black screen with sound effects. If they allow me to pour my own drink or make the dinner step by step, it would be more enjoyable.
